_raw_content: Your body held the answer before your mind formed the question. Every tightness is a truth you haven't let yourself speak. Every exhale that shakes is a wall coming down. You don't need to understand the wound to release it — you need to stop explaining it and start feeling it. The nervous system doesn't resolve through logic. It resolves through permission.
slide_1_hook: Your body solved it years ago — your mind just won't stop arguing.
slide_2: Before the thought, there was a sensation. Before the story, there was a contraction. Your body registered the truth the moment it happened. Then your mind built a cathedral of explanations on top of it so you'd never have to go back down.
slide_3: You are allowed to stop understanding your pain. You are allowed to let the shaking come without a narrative. You are allowed to feel something leave your body without knowing its name.
slide_4: Healing is not the moment you finally understand why. It is the moment your body exhales something it held for decades — and your mind doesn't rush in to label it. The shift happens below language. In the belly. In the jaw. In the hands that finally unclench.
slide_5: Feel your feet. Feel the weight of your body pressing into whatever holds you right now. That pressure is presence. That gravity is you arriving. You are not your spinning thoughts. You are the one who can feel them spin.
slide_6: I trust the intelligence of my body. I do not need to understand everything to release it. I am safe enough to feel what I have been explaining away.
caption:
Your mind wants to solve the body.

But the body was never the problem.

It was the faithful record keeper — holding every suppressed truth, every swallowed no, every moment you overrode yourself to stay safe.

The tightness in your chest is not dysfunction. It is data.
The knot in your throat is not anxiety. It is a voice.
The collapse in your gut is not weakness. It is an old surrender that never got to complete.

We have been taught that healing means understanding. That if we just find the right insight, the right origin story, the right framework — the pain will dissolve.

But the nervous system doesn't speak in paragraphs. It speaks in pressure. In temperature. In tremor.

And it resolves the same way.

Not through more thinking. Through more feeling.

The moment you stop narrating your experience and start inhabiting it — something ancient begins to move. Something that was waiting for you to stop explaining and start listening.

Your body knew first. It still knows. Let it finish what it started.
